message:
test(readme): cover Sparse Series + SparseAccessor (fd90.272 / br-lcmf8)

Extends readme_serialization_compiles_and_runs to cover the Sparse
extension dtype documented at README line 251:

- Series.from_sparse_dense(name, labels, values, dtype, fill_value)
  built a 5-element [1, 0, 0, 2, 0] sparse Series with fill_value=0.0
- Series.sparse() returning Option<SparseAccessor>
- sparse.value_dtype() — asserted Float64
- sparse.fill_value() — asserted &Scalar::Float64(0.0)
- sparse.npoints() — count of non-fill entries
- sparse.density() — npoints / len
- sparse.to_dense() — returns Series directly (no Result, no ?
  needed — caught during test write)

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-lbsx","title":"fp-types Timestamp string-unit rounding (9p0u Phase 2.6)","description":"Phase 2.6 follow-up to br-9p0u + br-5h6n. Pandas-style string-unit rounding on Timestamp + promote `Timedelta::unit_to_nanos` to public.

**Scope.**
- Promote `Timedelta::unit_to_nanos(unit)` from private to `pub`. Already implements the pandas alias set (W/D/H/m/s/ms/us/ns + word-form aliases).
- Add three string-unit rounding methods on Timestamp:
  - `Timestamp::floor_to_unit(unit_str) -> Timestamp`
  - `Timestamp::ceil_to_unit(unit_str) -> Timestamp`
  - `Timestamp::round_to_unit(unit_str) -> Timestamp`
- Each looks up `Timedelta::unit_to_nanos(unit_str)`; on unknown unit returns NaT (matches pandas ValueError surface as NaT propagation, consistent with the rest of fp-types).

**Reference (pandas 2.2):**
- `pd.Timestamp('2024-01-01 12:34:56').floor('H')` → 12:00:00.
- `.ceil('1H')` → 13:00:00.
- `.round('H')` → 13:00:00 (banker's tie-to-even).
- Unknown unit: pandas raises `ValueError: <X> is not a fixed frequency`.

**Fix shape (purely additive in fp-types):**
- 1 visibility change (`fn unit_to_nanos` → `pub fn unit_to_nanos`).
- 3 new pub fns on Timestamp delegating to the existing nanos-based variants.
- Tests covering pandas-canonical units + word forms + unknown unit → NaT.

**Blast radius.** No call-site touches (private→public is backwards-compat). Zero dep-graph change. fp-types stays chrono-free.

**Severity.** LOW — UX polish on top of 5h6n.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T06:08:31.785090190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T06:11:19.962871273Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T06:11:19.960395778Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","parity-gap","review","timestamp"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-lcmf8","title":"fd90.272: Cover Sparse Series + SparseAccessor methods","description":"fd90.272: Cover Sparse-typed Series + SparseAccessor methods.

The README at line 251 mentions "Sparse" as an extension dtype. Series.sparse() returns Option<SparseAccessor> (line 6742). SparseAccessor has 5 methods (value_dtype, fill_value, npoints, density, to_dense) at fp-frame:9669-9734. None integration-tested.

Plus Series.from_sparse_dense (line 6857) is the constructor for sparse Series — also untested.

Scope:
- Add a focused test or extend an existing one with:
  - Series.from_sparse_dense(...) to build a sparse Series
  - sparse() accessor → assert .value_dtype, .fill_value, .npoints, .density, .to_dense

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.

All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-lcuy2","title":"fd90.246: Cover GroupBy.quantile / transform_list / transform_fn / filter / apply","description":"fd90.246: Cover GroupBy.quantile / agg_dict_list / transform_list / transform_fn / filter / apply.

Several DataFrameGroupBy methods uncovered (relative to the impl block at line 26703+):
- gb.quantile(q: f64) — pandas df.groupby(...).quantile(0.5)
- gb.agg_dict_list — column -> list of agg-name dispatch
- gb.transform_list(&["sum", "mean"]) — list-of-string transforms
- gb.transform_fn(closure) — custom transform fn
- gb.filter(closure) — keep groups passing predicate
- gb.apply(closure) — closure returning DataFrame

Scope:
- Extend readme_groupby_aggregation_matrix with at least 4 of the most user-facing.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 20-30min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:08:24.210995173Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T06:10:43.712203724Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T06:10:43.710113184Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_groupby_aggregation_matrix_compiles_and_runs to cover 6 advanced GroupBy methods previously uncovered: gb.quantile(0.5), gb.transform_list(&['sum','mean']), gb.transform_fn (identity-closure transform), gb.filter (predicate keeping groups with >=2 rows), gb.apply (identity DataFrame closure), gb.apply_scalar (custom name + Scalar::Int64 group-count). All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ld8h","title":"fd90.45: gate escape_sql_ident / quote_sql_ident on sql-sqlite to silence no-default-features dead-code warnings","description":"Building fp-io with --no-default-features (i.e. without sql-sqlite) emits dead-code warnings for escape_sql_ident and quote_sql_ident — they're only used from the rusqlite SqlConnection impl which is cfg-gated to sql-sqlite. The helpers themselves are unconditional. Under cargo clippy --no-default-features, these warnings will trip clippy -D warnings if anyone runs that build profile in CI. Fix: add #[cfg(feature = "sql-sqlite")] to both helper definitions so they're only compiled when the feature is on. No external API change — these are private fns. Test: build with --no-default-features and verify no warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:43:01.783877140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:45:05.226496687Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:45:05.223978239Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","fd90","review","sql"]}
